subject: Databases
title: Amazon DynamoDB
description: A micro-lesson that makes Amazon DynamoDB usable.

hook.text: Amazon needed a database that never fails. DynamoDB was born from the Dynamo paper: always on, eventually consistent.

buildup.text: DynamoDB is a managed key-value and document store. You define a partition key and optional sort key.

discovery.text: It auto-scales capacity. You never manage servers, replication, or backups. AWS handles everything.

twist.text: DynamoDB offers both eventual and strongly consistent reads. Single-digit millisecond latency at any scale.

climax.text: Global tables replicate data across AWS regions. Multi-region writes with last-writer-wins conflict resolution.

punchline.text: DynamoDB: fully managed, infinitely scalable, always available.

quiz.question: How does DynamoDB handle multi-region writes with global tables?
options: It uses last-writer-wins conflict resolution across regions
It locks all regions during a write operation
